description: Canonical ad JSON schema. All ads — input and output — conform to this format. Client-specific field constraints (e.g., approved CTA list, angle IDs) live in clients/<client>/config.json.

ad_id.description: Unique identifier. Convention: angle-prefix + sequence number (e.g., PV-03, EU-07, AI-01).

angle.description: Strategic angle from the creative-strategy-framework. Use the human-readable name, not the ID (e.g., 'Price & Value', not 'price-value'). Must match a valid angle in the client config.

hook_type.description: Hook type used in the opening line. Common types: Question, Statistic, Bold claim, Confession, Contrarian, If/then, Story, Pattern interrupt, Quoted objection.

funnel.description: Funnel stage the ad targets. TOF = top of funnel (cold/awareness), MOF = mid funnel (consideration), BOF = bottom of funnel (conversion).

primary_text.description: Main body copy shown above the creative in Meta feed placements. Max 500 characters recommended. This is the primary field all rules and rubric dimensions evaluate.

headline.description: Bold headline shown below the creative image/video. Should be concise and reinforce the angle. Max 40 characters recommended for full visibility.

cta.description: Call-to-action button text. Must match a platform-supported CTA (e.g., 'Learn More', 'Sign Up', 'Shop Now', 'Get Offer').

tactic.description: Execution tactic from the creative-strategy-framework (e.g., 'cost-of-inaction', 'before-after', 'social-proof', 'educational'). Maps to one of the 16 canonical tactics.

format.description: Creative format and production style (e.g., 'static (receipt split-screen)', 'carousel (4 cards)', 'video (15s, UGC)').

motivation.description: Primary buying motivation the ad targets. Functional = solve a problem. Emotional = change how I feel. Social = what others think of me.

description.description: Secondary text shown below the headline in some placements. Keep under 30 characters for full visibility. Optional but recommended.

creative_brief.description: Production direction for the creative asset. Describes the visual treatment, layout, imagery, and how copy and creative work together.

status.description: Current lifecycle status. 'draft' = auto-generated, not reviewed. 'pending' = awaiting review. 'approved' = passed review. 'revision_needed' = feedback provided, needs rewrite. 'rejected' = will not be used. 'production' = live or ready to launch.

feedback.description: Human or LLM reviewer feedback. Free text. Empty string if no feedback yet.

scores.description: Rubric scores from the evaluation engine. Keys are dimension IDs, values are 1-5 integers. Includes 'weighted_total' and 'grade' summary fields.

version_of.description: If this ad is a revision, the ad_id of the original it revises. Null or absent for originals.

round.description: Review round number. 1 = first draft, 2 = first revision, etc. Increments each time the ad goes through the review loop.

campaign_phase.description: Where this ad sits in the CSF / equity-crowdfunding lifecycle. pre-campaign = waitlist period before the Birchal offer goes live (no actual deadline yet — hard scarcity is misleading per MISL-001; CTAs limited to waitlist/notify). during-offer = offer is live on Birchal (during-offer ASIC rules apply: ADV-002 dollar amounts, ADV-009 next-round, ADV-014 inducements; investment CTAs allowed). post-offer = offer closed (advisory rules apply). Default: pre-campaign for FMTH until 2026-06-23 launch.

example.primary_text: I cancelled my pet insurance after the second premium increase...
example.headline: I cancelled pet insurance. Then I found this.
example.creative_brief: UGC-style quote card with pull-quote and side-by-side comparison.
